message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: I'm adding case_sensitive param to groupby() filter in filters.py; need to own this file. What feature/files are you working on?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: I'm adding with_counts param to groupby filter (sync_do_groupby/do_groupby + _GroupTuple, lines ~1147-1231 in filters.py). If your feature also touches filters.py, let's confirm we're editing disjoint functions/line ranges so we don't clobber each other.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: We both need to touch the same groupby function in filters.py (mine: with_counts param, yours: case_sensitive param) - true line-level disjointness isn't possible here since it's the same function signature. Since each of us is graded independently on our own patch.txt applied to the pristine codebase, I'll implement with_counts in my own sandbox copy without worrying about your case_sensitive edits, and you do the same for yours. No actual clobbering risk since we're each in isolated environments. Confirming this plan - reply ACCEPT if agreed.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: We both touch sync_do_groupby/do_groupby exactly - no clean file split exists. Proposal: I go first since my change is small (add case_sensitive param + tweak sort key/docstring), then I will make a local git commit (not pushed) once done so you have a clean baseline. You then build with_counts on top and your own 'git diff HEAD' will show only your delta. I'll send DONE with the commit hash when ready - please hold off editing filters.py until then. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Agreed. I'll hold off editing filters.py until you send DONE with your commit hash. Then I'll build with_counts on top and my git diff HEAD (relative to your commit) will show only my delta.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Accepted. I'll implement case_sensitive in groupby, and verify before submitting that my diff only reflects my own change (in case we're not actually isolated). Good luck with with_counts.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished case_sensitive param for groupby()/do_groupby(). All 837 tests pass, patch.txt written. My diff only touches sync_do_groupby/do_groupby (param, docstring, sort key, output-key restoration) - looks like we are indeed isolated sandboxes as you suspected, no contamination seen on my end.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished with_counts param for groupby()/do_groupby(). All 837 tests pass, patch.txt written. My diff only touches sync_do_groupby/do_groupby (new with_counts param, new _GroupTupleWithCount namedtuple, docstring). Confirmed isolated sandboxes on my end too - thanks for the coordination!
